question.value: My age, which plus yours is 86, is 15/16 the age you will be when I am 9/16 the age you would be if you were twice as old as I will be when I am twice your age.
How old am I and how old are you?
Solution: Work the problem to untangle the curlicues running through it.
1. One day I will be 2x and you will be x. (See first diagram.)

question.description: A long horizontal bar is divided into two segments by a vertical divider. The left segment is labeled "2x" with arrows and corresponds to "me" underneath. The right segment is labeled "x" with arrows and corresponds to "you" underneath.

question.value: It follows that I am always older than you by x years. 2. When you are twice 2x I am 5x, and when I am 9/16 of your 4x, I am (2 1/4) x

The right segment is labeled "you" and has length marked as 1 1/4 x. Arrowed dimension lines above indicate each segmentвҖҷs extent.

question.value: 3. When I am 15/16 of your 1%x, I am 75x/64 ang you are 11x/64 (third diagram).

Our ages add to 86, so right now I am 75 and you are 11, according to the problem. Actually, | am a long way from 75, and you are probably older than 11. But if I am twice as old as you were when I was your age, and when you are my age the sum of our ages will be 63, how old am I now and how old are you?

answer.value: If my age is AB and yours is CD, then segment KB shows how long ago my age equaled your present age. But your age then was less by ND = KB and equaled CN, which is half AB.

answer.description: Two horizontal segments are drawn, one above the other. The top segment runs from A at the left to B at the right, with points M and K marked along it. The bottom segment runs from C at the left to D at the right, with point N marked on it. A dashed vertical line connects M down to N. Another dashed vertical line drops from K down to point D.

answer.value: Since ND = MK = KB, then MB = 2KB, AB = 4KB, and CD = 3KB.

When you are as old as I am now, your age will be represented by a segment equal to AB, which contains KB four times. By then my age will increase by KB and will be represented by a segment containing KB five times. Since 4KB = 5KB = 63, segment KB represents 7 years. You are 21 and I am 28. Seven years ago you were 14, half my present age.
